description: Session Rotation for Swarm — on second compaction or task boundary, rotate to a fresh session with structured checkpoint + board + memory bootstrap instead of degraded re-summarization.

userStories.title: Add rotation config group to config schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a rotation config section so that all rotation behavior can be toggled and tuned.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/config/config.ts, add a `rotation` group to the config schema with fields: enabled (boolean, default true), checkpoint_llm (boolean, default true), max_bootstrap_tokens (number, default 6000), conductor_defer (boolean, default true)
When rotation.enabled is false, the system falls back to compaction-only behavior (consumed by later stories)
Config supports project-level and global-level override (same as existing config groups)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Small config-only change. No runtime logic yet — just schema definition. Later stories read these values.

userStories.title: Session.archive() function
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to archive a session so that rotation can cleanly end old sessions while preserving them.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/index.ts, add an archive(sessionID) function to the Session namespace
archive() sets session.time.archived = Date.now() and persists to DB. Session schema already has time.archived as optional number
Define and publish a session.archived Bus event (add to Session.Event)
Archived sessions are still readable via Session.get() — no data is deleted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: time.archived field already exists in the schema. This story only adds the archive() function and event.

userStories.title: Block prompt injection into archived sessions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need SessionPrompt.prompt() to skip archived sessions so that no new messages enter a rotated-away session.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/prompt.ts, at the start of the prompt() function, fetch the session and check session.time.archived. If set, log a warning and return early without injecting any message
Existing callers (monitor relay, delegate_task, intervene) are unaffected when session is not archived
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (archive function). Simple guard clause addition.

userStories.title: Swarm.replaceWorkerSession() function
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to atomically replace a worker or conductor session reference in Swarm.Info when rotation occurs.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/swarm.ts, add replaceWorkerSession(swarmID, oldSessionID, newSessionID) to the Swarm namespace
Function loads Swarm.Info, finds the worker entry where session_id === oldSessionID and replaces it with newSessionID
If oldSessionID === Swarm.Info.conductor, update Swarm.Info.conductor to newSessionID instead
Copy SessionMetadata from old session to new session for keys: swarm_id, task_id, discussion_channel
Update Swarm.Info.time.updated and call Swarm.save()
Publish Swarm.Event.Updated event
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Signal watch routes by swarm_id not session_id, so monitor continues working without changes.

userStories.title: Checkpoint template generation (no LLM)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the template-based part of checkpoint generation that extracts goal, task, progress, and files from Board state without any LLM call.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/board/checkpoint.ts with a Checkpoint namespace

Define Checkpoint schema with zod: goal (string), task (string, task ID + subject), progress (array of { item: string, done: boolean }), decisions (array of string, default []), state (string, default empty), files (array of string), session_chain (array of string)
Implement generate(input: { sessionID, swarmID, taskID? }) that returns a Checkpoint content string in markdown format

Template fills goal from Swarm.Info.goal, task from current BoardTask (by taskID or first in_progress task), progress from BoardTask.list() mapping status to done/not-done, files from BoardArtifact.list() aggregating all artifact.files arrays
session_chain is built from SessionMetadata: walk prev_session chain backwards collecting IDs
If checkpoint_llm config is false, decisions and state remain empty (template-only mode)
Generated content is written as a Board artifact with type 'checkpoint' via BoardArtifact.post()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the zero-LLM-cost part. The LLM extraction for decisions/state is added in the next story.

userStories.title: Checkpoint LLM extraction for decisions and state
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LLM part of checkpoint generation that extracts key decisions and current working state from session history.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/board/checkpoint.ts, extend generate() to optionally run an LLM extraction step when config rotation.checkpoint_llm is true
LLM extraction reuses Compaction's message preparation: call MessageV2.toModelMessages(messages, model, { stripMedia: true, stripSynthetic: true }) to get the input
Use the existing compaction agent (Agent.get('compaction')) with a custom prompt that asks to extract: (1) key decisions made and their rationale, (2) current working state description
Parse the LLM response and fill the decisions[] and state fields in the checkpoint
If LLM extraction fails (timeout, error, etc.), gracefully degrade: keep decisions=[] and state='' from the template step, log a warning
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005. Adds the LLM layer on top of template generation. Failure is non-fatal.

userStories.title: Conductor checkpoint coordination extension
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Conductor checkpoints to include worker assignments, escalations, and progress stats.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/board/checkpoint.ts generate(), detect if session is Conductor by checking Swarm.Info.conductor === sessionID

When Conductor, append a <coordination> block to checkpoint content with: worker list and their task assignments (from Swarm.Info.workers), unresolved escalations (Board signals with type blocked/conflict that have no subsequent done/completed signal for the same task), overall progress (Board stats completed/total)
The <coordination> block is appended after the main checkpoint markdown, before artifact write
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005. Conductor-specific data is only relevant when the session being rotated is the Conductor.

userStories.title: Bootstrap prompt generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that assembles a concise bootstrap prompt from checkpoint, board state, and memories for a newly rotated session.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/rotation.ts, create a SessionRotation namespace and implement bootstrap(input: { checkpoint: string, swarmID: string, taskID?: string }) function

Bootstrap prompt assembles three XML blocks: <checkpoint> with the checkpoint content, <board_state> with Board snapshot summary (task stats, current task details, last 5 signals), <memories> with relevant memory entries recalled using keywords from checkpoint goal + task subject
Total prompt size is capped at max_bootstrap_tokens config value (default 6000) by truncating each block proportionally if over budget. Use Token.estimate() for measurement
Returns the assembled prompt string
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Does not inject the prompt — that is done by the rotate() function in a later story.

userStories.title: SessionRotation.rotate() core flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core rotation function that orchestrates the full sequence: memory extraction, checkpoint, archive, create, bootstrap, replace.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/rotation.ts, implement rotate(input: { sessionID, swarmID, trigger: 'overflow' | 'boundary' | 'manual' }) returning the new Session.Info

Execute steps in strict order: (1) run memory extraction via existing MemoryExtractor hooks (publish session.compacting event), (2) generate checkpoint via Checkpoint.generate(), (3) archive old session via Session.archive(), (4) create new session with same permission config copied from old session, (5) set SessionMetadata on new session: prev_session, rotation_seq (old seq + 1, default 0), plus copy swarm_id/task_id/discussion_channel from old session, (6) generate and inject bootstrap prompt via SessionPrompt.prompt() with the appropriate agent, (7) call Swarm.replaceWorkerSession()
Publish Bus events: session.rotation.started at step 1 and session.rotation.completed after step 7
If any step fails, log the error and return undefined to signal failure to caller
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (archive), US-004 (replace), US-005 (checkpoint), US-008 (bootstrap). This is the orchestrator.

userStories.title: shouldRotate() with Worker and Conductor logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single function that decides whether a session should rotate, with different rules for Worker and Conductor roles.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/rotation.ts, implement shouldRotate(input: { sessionID }) returning Promise<boolean>
Return false immediately if rotation.enabled config is false
Return false if session has no swarm_id in SessionMetadata (not a Swarm session)
Count summary messages: scan session message history for assistant messages with summary === true
If summary count is 0 (never compacted), return false
For Worker sessions (not Conductor): return true when summary count >= 1
For Conductor sessions (Swarm.Info.conductor === sessionID): if conductor_defer config is true AND Board snapshot stats.running > 0 AND summary count < 2, return false (defer); otherwise return true
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the decision function. Calling it has no side effects. Board snapshot read is cached (5s TTL).

userStories.title: Overflow trigger — integrate shouldRotate into prompt.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the session loop to check shouldRotate when overflow is detected and route to rotation instead of compaction.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/prompt.ts, in the session loop where isOverflow is checked and compaction is triggered, add a shouldRotate check before creating the compaction task

When isOverflow is true AND shouldRotate returns true: call SessionRotation.rotate({ sessionID, swarmID from metadata, trigger: 'overflow' }). If rotate succeeds (returns Session.Info), break the session loop. If rotate fails (returns undefined), fall through to normal compaction
When isOverflow is true AND shouldRotate returns false: proceed with existing compaction logic unchanged
Non-Swarm sessions are unaffected because shouldRotate returns false for them
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (rotate) and US-010 (shouldRotate). This is the main integration point.

userStories.title: Boundary trigger — rotate on task completion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Worker sessions to set a rotation flag when their assigned task completes, so the processor exits cleanly and rotation runs.
acceptanceCriteria: In the board_write tool handler, after a task status is updated to completed/failed/cancelled: check if session has swarm_id and task_id in SessionMetadata
When conditions are met, set a needsRotation flag on the processor context (similar to existing needsCompaction mechanism)
In src/session/processor.ts, handle the needsRotation flag: when set at finish-step, return 'rotate' as the process result (new enum value alongside 'continue', 'stop', 'compact')

In src/session/prompt.ts, handle the 'rotate' result from processor: call SessionRotation.rotate({ trigger: 'boundary' }) and break the loop. If rotation fails, log warning and continue (session can still be reused)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 (rotate). The needsRotation flag ensures the current step completes before rotation runs.

userStories.title: Rotation Bus events and structured logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need rotation events and logging for observability and debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/session/rotation.ts, define Bus events: session.rotation.started (payload: sessionID, swarmID, trigger, rotation_seq) and session.rotation.completed (payload: oldSessionID, newSessionID, checkpointArtifactID, bootstrap_tokens)

Add structured logging using Log.create({ service: 'session.rotation' }) for each rotation step: memory extraction, checkpoint generation (with duration), archive, session creation, bootstrap injection (with token count), reference replacement
On rotation failure, log the error at error level with step name and session context
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Events are published by rotate() in US-009. This story defines the event schemas and ensures logging is in place.
